items.title: Compute loss in log space
items.avoid: softmax(logits)[y] then -log(...) in manual training loops without stabilization.
items.prefer: F.cross_entropy(logits, target) or log_softmax + gather of −log π_y.
items.why: Log-sum-exp prevents underflow when π_y is tiny and keeps gradients numerically stable.
items.example: PyTorch: loss = F.cross_entropy(model(x), y) with raw logits from the final linear layer.

items.title: Match activation to label structure
items.avoid: Softmax output head when each example can have multiple correct labels.
items.prefer: Sigmoid per label with BCE for multi-label; softmax only for exactly-one-class problems.
items.why: Softmax enforces Σ π_k = 1, which is wrong when labels are independent Bernoulli events.
items.example: Product tags: K sigmoid outputs, not one softmax over 2^K combinations.

items.title: Use argmax on logits for prediction
items.avoid: Softmax then argmax when you only need the predicted class at inference.
items.prefer: argmax(z) directly — softmax is monotonic with respect to argmax.
items.why: Skipping softmax saves computation; probabilities are needed only when you report confidence or compute metrics.
items.example: Inference: pred = logits.argmax(dim=-1); report probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1) only for UI.
